<div class="quiz-shell edu-math-statistics-checkpoint" data-default-total="4"></div>

<script>
  (() => {
    const DEFAULT_TOPIC = "descriptive-statistics";
    const BANKS = {
      "descriptive-statistics": [meanQuestion, medianQuestion, iqrQuestion, outlierQuestion],
      sampling: [populationQuestion, biasQuestion, randomSampleQuestion, confoundingQuestion],
      "probability-for-statistics": [expectedValueQuestion, varianceQuestion, normalModelQuestion, binomialQuestion],
      "sampling-distributions": [standardErrorQuestion, cltQuestion, samplingDistributionQuestion, sampleSizeQuestion],
      estimation: [pointEstimateQuestion, confidenceIntervalQuestion, marginQuestion, confidenceMeaningQuestion],
      "hypothesis-tests": [nullQuestion, pValueQuestion, alphaQuestion, typeErrorQuestion],
      "comparing-groups": [pairedQuestion, independentQuestion, differenceQuestion, effectSizeQuestion],
      "chi-square-tests": [observedExpectedQuestion, goodnessFitQuestion, independenceQuestion, chiSquareCautionQuestion],
      "regression-basics": [slopeQuestion, residualQuestion, correlationQuestion, causationQuestion],
      "statistics-cumulative-review": [meanQuestion, biasQuestion, standardErrorQuestion, confidenceIntervalQuestion, pValueQuestion, slopeQuestion],
    };

    document.querySelectorAll(".edu-math-statistics-checkpoint:not([data-ready])").forEach(init);

    function init(root) {
      const config = readConfig(root);
      const state = { config, total: config.defaultTotal, index: 0, score: 0 };
      root.dataset.ready = "true";
      renderStart(root, state);
    }

    function readConfig(root) {
      const node = root.previousElementSibling;
      let parsed = {};
      if (node?.classList?.contains("edu-math-statistics-checkpoint-config")) {
        try {
          parsed = JSON.parse(node.textContent || "{}");
        } catch {
          parsed = {};
        }
      }
      return {
        topic: parsed.topic || root.dataset.topic || DEFAULT_TOPIC,
        title: parsed.title || "Statistics checkpoint",
        defaultTotal: Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(parsed.defaultTotal || 4))),
      };
    }

    function renderStart(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const input = element("input", {
        class: "quiz-count",
        type: "number",
        min: "1",
        max: "20",
        value: String(state.total),
      });
      const button = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Start checkpoint");
      button.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.total = Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(input.value) || 4));
        state.index = 0;
        state.score = 0;
        renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(
        element("p", { class: "quiz-topic" }, state.config.title),
        element("div", { class: "quiz-start" }, element("label", {}, element("span", {}, "Number of questions"), input), button),
      );
    }

    function renderQuestion(root, state, question) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      let answered = false;
      const progress = element(
        "div",
        { class: "quiz-progress" },
        element("span", {}, `Question ${state.index + 1} of ${state.total}`),
        element("span", {}, `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`),
      );
      const feedback = element("p", { class: "quiz-feedback", "aria-live": "polite" });
      const next = element("button", { type: "button", disabled: "" }, "Next question");
      const options = radioList(question.options, (selected) => {
        if (answered) return;
        answered = true;
        const correct = selected === question.answer;
        if (correct) state.score += 1;
        feedback.className = correct ? "quiz-feedback correct" : "quiz-feedback wrong";
        feedback.replaceChildren(correct ? "Correct." : `Not quite. The answer is ${question.answer}.`, " ", question.explanation);
        options.querySelectorAll("input").forEach((input) => {
          input.disabled = true;
        });
        progress.lastElementChild.textContent = `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`;
        next.disabled = false;
      });
      next.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.index += 1;
        if (state.index >= state.total) renderDone(root, state);
        else renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(progress, stimulus(question.stimulus), element("p", { class: "quiz-prompt" }, question.prompt), feedback, options, next);
    }

    function renderDone(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const restart = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Try again");
      restart.addEventListener("click", () => renderStart(root, state));
      root.append(element("h3", {}, "Checkpoint complete"), element("p", {}, `Final score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`), restart);
    }

    function makeQuestion(topic) {
      const bank = BANKS[topic] || BANKS[DEFAULT_TOPIC];
      return choice(bank)();
    }

    function meanQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "What is the mean of 2, 4, and 6?", stimulus: "add then divide by 3", answer: "4", distractors: ["3", "6", "12"], explanation: "(2+4+6)/3 = 4." });
    }
    function medianQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Which summary is most resistant to one very large outlier?", stimulus: "center with outliers", answer: "median", distractors: ["mean", "range", "maximum"], explanation: "The median depends on order position, not the exact size of the extreme value." });
    }
    function iqrQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "What does the IQR measure?", stimulus: "Q3 - Q1", answer: "spread of the middle half", distractors: ["average value", "sample size", "largest value only"], explanation: "IQR is the distance from the first quartile to the third quartile." });
    }
    function outlierQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A value far above the rest usually affects which summary most?", stimulus: "outlier", answer: "mean", distractors: ["median", "Q1", "sample size"], explanation: "The mean uses the exact size of every value." });
    }
    function populationQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "The full group we want to learn about is the...", stimulus: "study target", answer: "population", distractors: ["sample", "statistic", "response"], explanation: "A sample is selected from the population." });
    }
    function biasQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Surveying only gym visitors about exercise is mainly what problem?", stimulus: "who was selected", answer: "selection bias", distractors: ["random sampling", "larger sample size", "low variance"], explanation: "The selected group systematically differs from the target population." });
    }
    function randomSampleQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Why do random samples help?", stimulus: "chance selection", answer: "they reduce systematic selection patterns", distractors: ["they remove all uncertainty", "they prove causation", "they make every answer true"], explanation: "Random selection supports probability-based uncertainty estimates." });
    }
    function confoundingQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A hidden variable related to both treatment and outcome is called a...", stimulus: "causal caution", answer: "confounder", distractors: ["median", "residual", "margin of error"], explanation: "Confounding can create misleading associations." });
    }
    function expectedValueQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Expected value is best described as a...", stimulus: "weighted average", answer: "long-run average", distractors: ["guaranteed outcome", "sample maximum", "p-value"], explanation: "Expected value averages outcomes using probabilities as weights." });
    }
    function varianceQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Variance measures typical squared distance from the...", stimulus: "spread", answer: "mean", distractors: ["maximum", "sample size", "p-value"], explanation: "Variance averages squared deviations from the mean." });
    }
    function normalModelQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "The normal distribution is shaped like a...", stimulus: "symmetric model", answer: "bell", distractors: ["line", "triangle", "two-way table"], explanation: "Normal models are symmetric and bell-shaped." });
    }
    function binomialQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A binomial count records the number of...", stimulus: "fixed independent trials", answer: "successes", distractors: ["outliers", "residuals", "quartiles"], explanation: "Binomial variables count successes in a fixed number of independent trials." });
    }
    function standardErrorQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Standard error describes variation of a...", stimulus: "sample-to-sample", answer: "statistic", distractors: ["single raw value", "population label", "survey question wording"], explanation: "It is the standard deviation of a statistic's sampling distribution." });
    }
    function cltQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "The central limit theorem says sample means often become approximately...", stimulus: "large n", answer: "normal", distractors: ["categorical", "impossible", "biased"], explanation: "With conditions, the distribution of sample means is approximately normal." });
    }
    function samplingDistributionQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A sampling distribution is the distribution of...", stimulus: "repeat sampling", answer: "a statistic", distractors: ["one questionnaire", "a population name", "a graph title"], explanation: "Imagine calculating the statistic for many random samples." });
    }
    function sampleSizeQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "If sample size increases, standard error usually...", stimulus: "SE = s/sqrt(n)", answer: "decreases", distractors: ["increases", "stays forced at 1", "becomes a p-value"], explanation: "The square root of n is in the denominator." });
    }
    function pointEstimateQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A single-number estimate is a...", stimulus: "one value", answer: "point estimate", distractors: ["confidence level", "null hypothesis", "residual"], explanation: "A sample mean or sample proportion can be a point estimate." });
    }
    function confidenceIntervalQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A confidence interval is estimate plus/minus...", stimulus: "uncertainty", answer: "margin of error", distractors: ["sample maximum", "correlation", "confounder"], explanation: "The margin of error measures how far the interval extends from the estimate." });
    }
    function marginQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "For a 95% normal interval, the multiplier is about...", stimulus: "critical value", answer: "1.96", distractors: ["0.05", "10", "100"], explanation: "A two-sided 95% normal interval uses z* approximately 1.96." });
    }
    function confidenceMeaningQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "95% confidence describes the long-run success rate of the...", stimulus: "repeated samples", answer: "method", distractors: ["single fixed interval", "null being true", "largest observation"], explanation: "The confidence level belongs to the procedure over repeated samples." });
    }
    function nullQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "The baseline claim in a test is the...", stimulus: "H0", answer: "null hypothesis", distractors: ["sample statistic", "residual", "histogram"], explanation: "The null often represents no change, no effect, or an existing claim." });
    }
    function pValueQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A p-value is computed assuming which hypothesis is true?", stimulus: "test logic", answer: "null hypothesis", distractors: ["alternative hypothesis", "largest value", "regression line"], explanation: "It measures how surprising the data are under the null model." });
    }
    function alphaQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "If p-value <= alpha, the usual decision is to...", stimulus: "decision rule", answer: "reject the null", distractors: ["prove the null", "ignore the data", "increase sample bias"], explanation: "The result is statistically significant at that alpha level." });
    }
    function typeErrorQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Rejecting a true null hypothesis is a Type...", stimulus: "false positive", answer: "I error", distractors: ["II error", "residual", "quartile"], explanation: "Type I error is a false positive decision." });
    }
    function pairedQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Before-and-after measurements on the same people are...", stimulus: "same units twice", answer: "paired", distractors: ["independent", "categorical only", "a histogram"], explanation: "Pairing means observations are naturally linked." });
    }
    function independentQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Two separate randomly selected groups are usually treated as...", stimulus: "different units", answer: "independent samples", distractors: ["paired samples", "one residual", "one population parameter"], explanation: "No observation in one group is matched to a specific observation in the other." });
    }
    function differenceQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "When comparing two means, the estimate is often...", stimulus: "group A minus group B", answer: "a difference", distractors: ["a maximum", "a p-value only", "a sample label"], explanation: "The difference in sample means estimates the difference in population means." });
    }
    function effectSizeQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Practical importance is mostly about the...", stimulus: "real-world size", answer: "effect size", distractors: ["file name", "random seed", "graph color"], explanation: "A tiny effect can be statistically significant but not practically important." });
    }
    function observedExpectedQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Chi-square statistics compare observed counts to...", stimulus: "null model", answer: "expected counts", distractors: ["sample means", "residual plots", "confidence levels"], explanation: "Expected counts are what the null model predicts for each category." });
    }
    function goodnessFitQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A goodness-of-fit test uses how many categorical variables?", stimulus: "one-way table", answer: "one", distractors: ["two", "three slopes", "no counts"], explanation: "It compares one categorical distribution to a claimed distribution." });
    }
    function independenceQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A chi-square test of independence uses a...", stimulus: "two categorical variables", answer: "two-way table", distractors: ["scatterplot only", "sample mean", "slope formula"], explanation: "Rows and columns represent the two categorical variables." });
    }
    function chiSquareCautionQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Chi-square methods are based on...", stimulus: "categorical data", answer: "counts", distractors: ["raw angles", "regression slopes only", "derivatives"], explanation: "The data should be frequency counts in categories." });
    }
    function slopeQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "In regression, slope is the predicted change in y for...", stimulus: "one-unit change", answer: "one more unit of x", distractors: ["one more residual", "the median only", "a smaller p-value"], explanation: "Slope connects changes in the explanatory variable to predicted changes in the response." });
    }
    function residualQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A residual is observed y minus...", stimulus: "prediction error", answer: "predicted y", distractors: ["mean x", "sample size", "confidence level"], explanation: "Residuals show prediction errors for individual points." });
    }
    function correlationQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "Correlation is always between...", stimulus: "r", answer: "-1 and 1", distractors: ["0 and 100", "1 and infinity", "only positive values"], explanation: "Pearson correlation ranges from -1 to 1." });
    }
    function causationQuestion() {
      return question({ prompt: "A strong regression association alone proves causation?", stimulus: "causal caution", answer: "no", distractors: ["yes", "always", "only if slope is positive"], explanation: "Confounding and study design matter for causal claims." });
    }

    function question({ prompt, stimulus, answer, distractors, explanation }) {
      return { prompt, stimulus, answer, options: shuffle([answer, ...distractors]), explanation };
    }
    function radioList(options, onSelect) {
      const name = `statistics-checkpoint-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
      const container = element("div", { class: "quiz-options" });
      options.forEach((option) => {
        const input = element("input", { type: "radio", name, value: option });
        input.addEventListener("change", () => onSelect(option));
        container.append(element("label", {}, input, element("span", {}, option)));
      });
      return container;
    }
    function stimulus(text) {
      return element("pre", { class: "quiz-stimulus" }, text);
    }
    function element(tag, attrs = {}, ...children) {
      const node = document.createElement(tag);
      Object.entries(attrs).forEach(([key, value]) => node.setAttribute(key, value));
      node.append(...children);
      return node;
    }
    function choice(values) {
      return values[Math.floor(Math.random() * values.length)];
    }
    function shuffle(values) {
      const copy = values.slice();
      for (let index = copy.length - 1; index > 0; index -= 1) {
        const other = Math.floor(Math.random() * (index + 1));
        [copy[index], copy[other]] = [copy[other], copy[index]];
      }
      return copy;
    }
  })();
</script>
